hook.text: One primary (leader) accepts all writes. Followers copy data from the leader. Reads can go to any follower.

buildup.text: The leader streams a replication log to followers. Each follower replays it to stay in sync.

discovery.text: This is the most common setup. Postgres streaming replication, MySQL replication, and MongoDB replica sets all use it.

twist.text: Replication lag means followers may be a few milliseconds behind the leader. Stale reads are possible.

climax.text: If the leader fails, a follower is promoted. This failover can be automatic or manual.

punchline.text: Leader-follower: one writer, many readers. Simple and widely used.

quiz.question: What is replication lag?
options: The delay between a write on the leader and its appearance on a follower
The time it takes to create a new replica
The delay in processing a query on the leader
